title: Cleansing of Kashyyyk

{{Quote|Many years ago, when I was far younger than I am now, a Jedi came to hunt the creature… a proud and boastful young man. Like many before him, he did not return.|translated=1|[[Freyyr]], to Revan, regarding Guun Han Saresh|<ref name="kotor">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
In [[3993 BBY]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Guun Han Saresh]] ventured to the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] to '''hunt''' a "great beast" that was rumored to reside in the planet's [[Shadowlands/Legends|Shadowlands]]. During the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]], a collaborative [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] effort to rid [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-created monstrosities in the aftermath of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], Saresh had established himself as one of the very best [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] in the entire [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. As such, he was handpicked by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] for a secret mission to rid the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] of the vicious [[Terentatek/Legends|terentatek]] beast. However, Saresh left the group and instead went to hunt the rumored "great beast" of Kashyyyk, which he believed to be a terentatek. After setting a trap for the beast in the Shadowlands, Saresh attacked the terentatek with his [[Force-imbued sword/Legends|Force-imbued blade]]—unfortunately for Saresh, the [[Sword/Legends|sword]] shattered on his prey's tough hide, and the defenseless Jedi was [[Death/Legends|eaten alive]].

==Prelude==
{{Quote|During my time on Korriban, I heard of a 'great beast' that lived in the ''Shadowlands'', beneath the mile-high ''wroshyr'' trees of Kashyyyk. The Wookiees tell me that the beast has killed many Jedi. It can only be ''one thing''.|Guun Han Saresh|<ref name="shadows" />}}
In [[3996 BBY]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lords]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] launched the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] against the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Although the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] were eventually defeated, [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemically]]-created monstrosities that had been bred by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] users still survived on [[Planet/Legends|planets]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and threatened both the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the general populace. In response, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] organized the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]], a collaborative effort to eliminate these beasts wherever they tread.<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Over the next three [[Standard year/Legends|years]], one particular group of [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] that included [[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Duron Qel-Droma]] and [[Shaela Nuur]], along with [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Guun Han Saresh]], established itself as the best the Order had to offer. After the team [[Cleansing of Tatooine|cleansed]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] of the vicious and highly dangerous [[Terentatek/Legends|terentatek]] beast, an official end was called to the Great Hunt. However, the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] selected Qel-Droma, Nuur, and Saresh for a special, secret mission—to exterminate terentateks on the Sith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], a world deeply steeped in the dark side.<ref name="shadows" />

The Korriban mission did not go according to plan. Saresh, disapproving of the budding romance between Qel-Droma and Nuur, eventually left the group, traveling to the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] world of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] in order to hunt a "great beast" that he had heard lived in the planet's [[Shadowlands/Legends|Shadowlands]], which he believed to be a terentatek. After arriving on Kashyyyk and investigating the "great beast," Saresh became convinced that it existed, and descended into the Shadowlands in order to begin the hunt.<ref name="shadows" />

==The cleansing==
{{Quote|The hunt goes on.|Guun Han Saresh|<ref name="shadows" />}}
Once in the dark Shadowlands, Saresh decided to set a trap for the terentatek. This he accomplished by using his [[Crossbow/Legends|crossbow]] to slay an [[Bird/Legends|avian]] [[Mykal/Legends|mykal]] beast, which he hung in the air with some vines. Saresh retreated to a high rock overlook and waited for the "great beast" to arrive—soon enough, the terentatek came to feast on the mykal. Not seeing Saresh emerge from his perch, the terentatek was on the receiving end of a surprise attack from Saresh's [[Force-imbued sword/Legends|Force-imbued blade]]. However, to Saresh's horror, the Jedi's [[Sword/Legends|sword]] was shattered at the hilt by the terentatek's thick hide. Left defenseless, Saresh was [[Death/Legends|devoured]] by the beast.<ref name="shadows" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Did you… did you feel that? Something in the Force… he… no… Guun Han—he's ''gone''.|Duron Qel-Droma senses Guun Han Saresh's death|<ref name="shadows" />}}
Although the Jedi Council was relatively certain that Saresh had died, it had no knowledge about his exact fate, since it was too dangerous to send someone out to find his body.<ref name="kotor" /> The same [[Standard day/Legends|day]] that Saresh perished on Kashyyyk, his former compatriots Qel-Droma and Nuur were also slain by terentateks in Korriban's [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]].<ref name="shadows" /> In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="nec" /> thirty-seven [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the death of Guun Han Saresh, the Jedi [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] killed the terentatek that had claimed the Twi'lek's life on Kashyyyk, finding Saresh's [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and his [[Circlet of Saresh/Legends|Circlet of Saresh]] in the process.<ref name="kotor" />

==Behind the scenes==
The battle on Kashyyyk was first mentioned in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''.<ref name="kotor" /> The event first appeared in "[[Shadows and Light]]," a story published in [[Star Wars Tales 23|''Star Wars Tales'' 23]], that was written by [[Joshua Ortega]], illustrated by [[Dustin Weaver]],<ref name="shadows" /> and released on [[May 11]], [[2005]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54696/star_wars_tales_1999_23|text=Star Wars Tales (1999) #23}}</ref>
